findings.evidence: `rg -n "apply_symm_apply|symm_apply_apply"` shows Mathlib names `e.symm (e x) = x` as `symm_apply_apply`; this lemma is at lines 122-125.
findings.issue: `orbitQuotientEquivBase_apply_symm` proves `hreg.orbitQuotientEquivBase.symm (hreg.orbitQuotientEquivBase x) = x`, so the name advertises the wrong composition order.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "apply_symm_apply|symm_apply_apply"` shows Mathlib names `e (e.symm y) = y` as `apply_symm_apply`; this lemma is at lines 129-131.
findings.issue: `orbitQuotientEquivBase_symm_apply` proves `hreg.orbitQuotientEquivBase (hreg.orbitQuotientEquivBase.symm b) = b`, so the name advertises the wrong composition order.

summary: Two inverse-law simp lemmas use names opposite to the standard Mathlib `apply_symm_apply`/`symm_apply_apply` convention.
